channel.title: The Car Jury, News
channel.description: News and verdicts on the Indian car market from The Car Jury.

item.title: Next-gen BMW M3 keeps the manual: enthusiasts, exhale
item.description: BMW M boss Frank van Meel confirms the next-gen ICE M3 will retain a manual gearbox. The Car Jury on why this matters for India's enthusiast pool.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG M9 and Cyberster Couture Editions: fashion tax on 50 lucky buyers
item.description: MG opens bookings for M9 and Cyberster Couture Editions by Gaurav Gupta. Fifty units each, prices on August 3. The Car Jury's verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti's 39% share is the price of ignoring what Indians actually want
item.description: Maruti Suzuki's market share slips to around 39% as Tata and Mahindra capitalise on India's SUV shift and its feature-lean strategy backfires.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Renames Nexon As 'Osprey' For South Africa, Launch In Q3 2026
item.description: Tata Motors will sell the Nexon as the 'Osprey' in South Africa from Q3 2026. Same SUV, new nameplate inspired by the bird of prey.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Hyundai's Bayon-based SUV Targets Victoris With NA Petrol and Underbody CNG
item.description: Hyundai's upcoming Bayon-based SUV will launch with a 1.5 NA petrol, followed by a CNG variant with underbody tank and spare wheel, targeting the Maruti Victoris.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Punch Hits 8 Lakh Sales: The Punch Is Now Tata's Second Million-Bound SUV
item.description: Tata Punch crosses 8 lakh domestic sales in under five years, powered by petrol, CNG and EV demand. TCJ on why it works.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Avinya Spied On Indian Roads: Premium EV Flagship Inches Closer To 2027
item.description: Tata Avinya premium electric SUV has been spied testing in India for the first time, previewing the brand's 2027 EV flagship push.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: myHyundai App Opens 30,000+ Charging Points To All EVs, Not Just Hyundais
item.description: Hyundai's myHyundai app now aggregates 30,000+ EV charging points across India, open to all brands, with slot booking and digital payments built in.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Reva at 25: Chetan Maini's EV Legacy, and Why India Owes Him More Than a Nod
item.description: India's first electric car turns 25. Founder Chetan Maini says he would never say no to another EV venture. Our take on his legacy.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia Syros EV: Seven Variants, Two Batteries, One Real Rival Problem
item.description: Kia Syros EV lands in seven variants with 42 kWh and 51.4 kWh batteries, taking on the Nexon EV and XUV 3XO EV from around Rs 15 lakh.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Innova Hycross Facelift Spied: Projector Lights and Sharper Cabin Coming
item.description: Toyota Innova Hycross facelift spied testing with projector headlamps, revised grille and interior tweaks. Launch expected late 2026 or 2027.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Range Rover Sport Electric Breaks Cover At Goodwood, India Waits Till 2027
item.description: Range Rover Sport Electric revealed at Goodwood with 550hp and a 118.5kWh battery. Global sale from September 2026, India launch by March 2027.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Ferrari Amalfi Spider lands in India at Rs 4.6 crore, four months after global debut
item.description: Ferrari's Amalfi Spider arrives in India at Rs 4.6 crore before options, packing the coupe's 640hp twin-turbo V8 and a folding fabric roof.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG Bets Rs 1,400 Cr on ADAPT Platform: Two New-Energy SUVs Land in FY27
item.description: JSW MG Motor commits Rs 1,400 crore to launch a battery-electric and plug-in hybrid SUV on its new ADAPT platform by FY27. Our take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: JSW MG's Wuling-based electric SUV lands in August to undercut Mahindra XEV 9S
item.description: JSW MG will launch an Eksion-based electric SUV in August 2026 with a 69kWh pack and 600km range, priced to undercut the Mahindra XEV 9S 70kWh.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Toyota Teases 9th-Gen Hilux Ahead Of 28 July India Launch
item.description: Toyota has teased the new 9th-gen Hilux ahead of its India launch on 28 July 2026, likely still with the 2.8 diesel and no hybrid.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Volvo EX90 lands in India on August 19: flagship EV, niche audience
item.description: Volvo EX90 debuts in India on August 19, 2026 with an 800V platform, up to 680hp and 608km WLTP range. Our verdict on where it fits.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra Chakan Hits 3 Million Units: The BE 6 Rolls Off As Proof Of Pivot
item.description: Mahindra's Chakan facility has built its three-millionth vehicle, a BE 6 EV. The pace of the last million tells the real story.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG Bets 80% of Sales on EVs as Fuel Prices Spike Post West Asia War
item.description: JSW MG Motor India MD Anurag Mehrotra expects NEVs to make up 70-80% of sales this year, riding fuel price volatility after the West Asia war.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Raipur Consumer Court Orders Maruti to Replace Grand Vitara Over E20 Damage
item.description: Raipur consumer court directs Maruti Suzuki to replace a 2023 Grand Vitara Zeta+ over E20-related damage, or refund Rs 20.5 lakh with penalties.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG's ADAPT Platform Is A Smart Bet, But India Still Needs Proof
item.description: MG Motor India reveals ADAPT, a flexible platform for PHEVs, strong hybrids, range-extender hybrids and pure EVs, skipping pure-ICE cars entirely.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: NHTSA Weighs Probe Into 806,963 Honda Odyssey Minivans Over Airbag Deployment
item.description: US regulator NHTSA is reviewing a petition to probe 806,963 Honda Odyssey minivans from 2011-2017 over inadvertent airbag deployment concerns.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia Syros EV Bookings Open: 526 km Range, Two Battery Packs, Seven Variants
item.description: Kia opens Syros EV bookings at Rs 25,000 with 42 kWh and 51.4 kWh battery options, up to 526 km range and seven variants. The Car Jury's take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG Hikes Cyberster and M9 Prices Again: Up To Rs 5 Lakh Dearer In Three Months
item.description: MG has raised prices of the M9 limousine and Cyberster EV by up to Rs 5 lakh in July 2026, the second hike this year at MG Select stores.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Victoris Dethrones Creta: 4.2-4.5m SUV Sales June 2026 Rewrite the Order
item.description: Maruti Victoris leads the 4.2-4.5m SUV segment in June 2026 with 10,035 units. Seltos second, Creta crashes to fifth. Full sales verdict inside.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: New Lexus ES 350h at Rs 66.10 lakh: the sensible Lexus is back
item.description: Lexus launches the eighth-gen ES 350h hybrid in India from Rs 66.10 lakh, undercutting the ES 500e EV by nearly Rs 18 lakh. TCJ's take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Volkswagen ID Cross Debuts: Compact EV Crossover Skips India Again
item.description: Volkswagen unveils the ID Cross, its first compact electric crossover on the MEB+ platform, priced from EUR 27,995. India relevance is another matter.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: HPCL Claims Zero Adulteration After 2,173 Ethanol Blend Checks
item.description: HPCL completes 2,173 surprise inspections at retail outlets between July 7-13, 2026, and reports zero ethanol-blending or adulteration lapses.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra Storms Past MG in H1 2026 EV Sales; Maruti Debuts at Fourth
item.description: Mahindra jumps to India's number two EV maker with 147 percent growth, pushing MG to third. Tata still leads. Maruti debuts fourth.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Xiaomi's SkyNomad N90 Is A 5.3m Range-Extender SUV Chasing Volvo Cues
item.description: Xiaomi's new SkyNomad sub-brand debuts the N90, a 5.3m three-row range-extender SUV with Volvo-style DRLs and LiDAR. Here is what it means.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia Carens Clavis EV: Rs 1.8 lakh off in July, but only on MY25 stock
item.description: Kia is discounting the Carens Clavis EV by up to Rs 1.8 lakh in July 2026. The big money is on MY25 stock, not the fresh cars.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: VW Group to axe half its models by 2030: what it means for India
item.description: Volkswagen Group will cut its global model line-up by up to 50 percent and variants by 75 percent by 2030. Here is what India should expect.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra June 2026: Scorpio, Thar, XUV 3XO Do The Heavy Lifting
item.description: Mahindra sold 60,393 SUVs in June 2026, up 27.66% YoY. Scorpio, Thar and XUV 3XO lead; the electric 3XO turbocharges growth.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Renault Kiger gets Rs 1.25 lakh off in July 2026: buy signal or clearance?
item.description: Renault Kiger carries benefits up to Rs 1.25 lakh in July 2026, the steepest in the range. Here is what the discount actually says about the car.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata June 2026: Punch, Nexon, Sierra Push Tata to 62,076 Units, Up 67% YoY
item.description: Tata sold 62,076 PVs in June 2026, up 67.40% YoY. Punch led with 21,006 units, Nexon followed at 18,335, Sierra added fresh volume.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Sierra EV Launched From Rs 18.79 Lakh: Priced To Kill Its Own Petrol Sibling
item.description: Tata Sierra EV launches from Rs 18.79 lakh ex-showroom, undercutting rivals and matching the Sierra petrol AT. Our verdict on whether it earns the badge.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Battery-as-a-Service Cuts EV Sticker Prices, But Raises Lifetime Cost
item.description: Tata, Hyundai and Maruti are pushing Battery-as-a-Service to slash EV sticker prices, but the lifetime bill for the buyer is going up, not down.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia Syros EV Caught Undisguised in Bengaluru: Launch is Close
item.description: Kia Syros EV spotted completely undisguised during a TVC shoot in Bengaluru. Closed grille, boxy silhouette confirmed. Launch imminent.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Sierra Jubilee Edition: Smart Sweetener, Not a Reason to Buy
item.description: Tata marks 50,000 Sierra sales with a Jubilee Edition on Smart+, Pure and Adventure trims, adding ROQ accessories and a few missing features.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mercedes-AMG E 53 PHEV lands July 23: 585hp, 100km EV range, one very expensive science project
item.description: Mercedes-AMG launches the E 53 HYBRID 4MATIC+ in India on July 23 with a 3.0-litre six, 585hp combined and over 100km EV range.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Volkswagen's China Collapse Drags Global Sales to 4-Year Low: India Buyers Unaffected
item.description: Volkswagen's Q2 2026 deliveries fell 8.6 percent globally as China market shrank 20 percent. What it means for Indian VW buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Hyundai June 2026 Sales: Venue Tops Chart, Creta Slips as Supplier Fire Hits Output
item.description: Hyundai India June 2026 sales fell 10% YoY to 39,635 units after a supplier fire. Venue overtook Creta as the brand's top-seller.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Sierra Jubilee Edition: An Accessory Pack With A Fancy Name, From Rs 11.99 Lakh
item.description: Tata launches Sierra Jubilee Edition from Rs 11.99 lakh with three dealer-fit accessory packs. The Car Jury on whether it changes the Sierra's case.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia Syros EV: 520km Range, Rs 15-20 Lakh Price Puts Nexon EV On Notice
item.description: Kia Syros EV gets 42kWh and 51.4kWh batteries from the Carens Clavis EV, up to 520km ARAI range. Bookings open, launch expected this month.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Nissan Can Still Build Its Own Cars At Renault's Chennai Plant
item.description: Nissan confirms it can build vehicles on its own platforms at the Renault-run RNAIPL Chennai plant, despite exiting the manufacturing JV last year.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia Sorento Bookings Open at Rs 25,000: Flagship Hybrid SUV Lands in October
item.description: Kia has unofficially opened Sorento bookings in India at Rs 25,000. A 1.6 hybrid, three seating layouts, and an October 2026 launch are on the cards.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG Shows GO Hatch and Cyber SUV at Goodwood: Europe First, India Later
item.description: MG unveils the GO electric hatchback and Cyber SUV concepts at Goodwood 2026. Europe is the priority; India gets these later, if at all.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: VW Tayron Life at Rs 41.99 Lakh: Cheaper Tayron, But Still a Hard Sell
item.description: Volkswagen Tayron Life launches at Rs 41.99 lakh ex-showroom, a 5-seat, softer-styled sibling to the R-Line with the same engine. TCJ's verdict inside.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Centre Kills Pure Petrol and E10 Choice: E20 Is Now Your Only Option
item.description: The Centre has ruled out selling pure petrol, E10 and E20 side by side, locking E20 as India's default petrol blend. Here is what it means.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Centre Orders States To Crack Down On Fuel Adulteration Amid E20 Backlash
item.description: The Centre has directed states to take iron-hand action against fuel adulteration, separating pump-level lapses from the E20 petrol debate.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)
